notes: Reverse-translating ('the engineer whose decision-making is no longer pulled by divergent external claims AND no longer internally re-litigating after each pull is the buddhi the verse names; this is the closing condition, after which yoga is attained') yields a stance compatible with all four traditions. Shankara's gloss (niścalā = no external pull, acalā = no internal doubt; samādhau = in the Self) is preserved structurally. Ramanuja's eka-vṛtti gloss is consistent. The pivot foreshadowing (2.54 onwards) is a structural-textual claim that all traditions agree on. No distortion. Pass.
